## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On May 17, 1954, the incredibly massive, absolute watershed legal decision of the United States Supreme Court in *Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka* was officially and unanimously delivered. Chief Justice Earl Warren aggressively authored the massive ruling, fundamentally declaring that state laws establishing separate public schools for black and white students were completely and absolutely unconstitutional. This massive legal triumph heavily shattered the horrific "separate but equal" doctrine, aggressively catalyzing the massive American Civil Rights Movement.

On May 17, 1792, the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) was officially and aggressively founded by massive groups of influential stockbrokers who heavily signed the absolute, historic Buttonwood Agreement on Wall Street. This massive, foundational financial pact aggressively created a highly centralized, deeply regulated exchange market for securities trading, heavily laying the absolute, monumental architectural groundwork for what would eventually become the largest, most massive, and incredibly powerful financial institution in the history of global capitalism.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)

On May 17, 1540, the incredibly massive, highly decisive Battle of Kannauj (also known as the Battle of Bilgram) was aggressively fought between the massive forces of the Mughal Emperor Humayun and the incredibly brilliant Afghan leader Sher Shah Suri. Sher Shah heavily and aggressively routed the massive Mughal army, forcing Humayun into absolute, devastating exile in Persia for 15 years. This massive victory heavily allowed Sher Shah to aggressively establish the formidable Sur Empire across northern India, heavily interrupting the massive Mughal dynasty.

On May 17, 1974, Leela Row Dayal, a massive, highly influential Indian tennis player, deeply revered classical dancer, and incredibly accomplished author, passed away. She achieved absolute, historic sporting immortality by heavily becoming the very first Indian female tennis player to aggressively win a match at the massive Wimbledon Championships in 1934. Her massive, multifaceted cultural contributions heavily advanced both the athletic and massive, traditional artistic presence of Indian women on the absolute, highly competitive global stage.
